rulings.Case Title: JAVIER REYES VS. JANICE RODGERS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT JANICE RODGERS' Motion To Set Aside Default/Default Judgment/Leave To Defend. Judge Miller recuses herself from this case. Per order of the presiding judge, the motion is continued to July 9, 2014, to give the moving party a chance to comply with San Francisco Local Rule 2.6B. Please immediately provide a courtesy copy of the moving papers with a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT LEUNG VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Ntc Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 2, PETITIONER ROBERT LEUNG's (Amended) Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandate. Continued to August 12, 2014 for the following reasons: (1) The Court is not in receipt of Courtesy Copies of the Petition filed on December 20, 2011 and the Memorandum of Points and Authorities filed on November 8, 2013. Petitioner must deliver courtesy copies of these papers to Dept. 302 no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date with a cover letter stating the new hearing date. (See Local Rule 2.6B.) (2) The Administrative Record is comprised of 95 exhibits, but the exhibits are not separated by exhibit tabs, as required by CRC 3.1110(f). The Index to Administrative Record does not identify the bates range for each exhibit. Petitioner must deliver an administrative record with exhibit tabs, and a cover letter reflecting the new hearing date, no later than two weeks prior to the new hearing date. Petitioner may pick up the administrative record that Dept. 302 is currently in receipt of if he wishes. Please contact the Court Clerk at (415) 551-3723 to make arrangements for pick up. (3) The Court requests that Petitioner ensure that all hearing transcripts have been provided to the Court. The Court notes that it has received a CD containing audio recording of a June 16, 2010 hearing, but there does not appear to be a corresponding transcript in the Administrative Record. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES P. DIWIK VS. PRODUCTS INNOVATION GROUP, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Motion To Correct And Confirm The Attorney-Client Fee Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 3, PETITIONER JAMES DIWIK's Motion To Correct And Confirm The Attorney-Client Fee Arbitration Award And Enter Judgment Thereon. The motion to correct and confirm the attorney-client fee arbitration award is granted. No opposition filed. The Court also awards costs in the requested amount of $1,037.82 and reasonable attorneys' fees in the amount of $3200. The Court will sign the proposed order and judgment submitted with the moving papers, in the total amount of $32,869.68 to reflect the Court's reduction in the request for attorneys fees. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 4, PETITIONERs KAISER FOUNDATION HOSPITALS, KAISER FOUNDATION HEALTH PLAN, INC., THE PERMANENTE MEDICAL GROUP, INC.'s Petition To Appoint A Neutral Arbitrator. The Petition to Appoint a Neutral Arbitrator is granted. No opposition was filed. Each party is required to serve and file a list of not more than five proposed arbitrator candidates by July 11, 2014. If the parties cannot agree on an arbitrator from this list, a party may make an ex parte application (with notice) for the appointment of an arbitrator pursuant to CCP §1281.6. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ELVIA JUAREZ VS. ANTHONY E NOVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Evidence Issue And Terminating Discovery Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ELVIA JUAREZ's Motion For Evidence Issue And Terminating Discovery Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: RICHARD FRAIGE VS. MATTHEW T. KESSLER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton To Compel Pltf To Provide Discovery Responses, Documents And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 2, CROSS COMPLAINANT MATTHEW KESSLER's Motion To Compel Plaintiff To Provide Discovery Responses, Documents And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Appearance required. The Court believes that there were insufficient meet and confer efforts made to resolve the discovery at issue and is inclined to continue the motion for approximately 30 days and order further discussions between counsel. The Court will address this issue, and others raised by the moving and opposition papers, at the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: YAHOR ZGURSKI VS. SERGEY GRECHKO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Defts Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 3, PLAINTIFF YAHOR ZGURSKI's Motion For Order Compelling Defendants Responses To Demand For Production Of Documents And Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: No opposition filed; motion granted. Sanctions of $750 imposed against Defendants. The Court notes that a few of the outstanding requests (Interrogatory No. 12; RFPD Nos. 2, 10, 13, 55) potentially implicate the financial privacy rights of third parties. As to these requests only, this order is without prejudice to a subsequent motion to assure that the privacy rights of these individuals are properly protected if this information is produced. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davidmcdonald@prodigy.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX G FLORES VS. EXECUPARK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Monday, June 23, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT JOSE ZELAYA's Motion To Strike 1st Amended Complaint. Hearing required. This matter originally came on for hearing on May 21, 2014. Plaintiff did not file any opposition. The Court continued the matter to June 23, 2014, and ordered the defendant to lodge courtesy copies of its filings with the court. Defendant provided those papers and the proof of service indicates that defendant properly served plaintiff with the initial papers. The Court received a document dated June 17, 2014 entitled "Declaration of No Notice of Hearing Re: Zelaya's Demurrer and Motion to Strike" from plaintiff's counsel stating that he did not receive notice of the June 23, 2014, hearing date. This declaration has apparently not been filed with the Court, but appears to have been served on counsel.. It is unclear whether plaintiff received copies of defendant's filings and/or whether plaintiff intends to file an opposition. =(302/MJM)
